seoTitle: How to Add Typeform Responses to Microsoft Excel
metaDescription: Learn how to connect Typeform to Excel so new form submissions automatically populate your spreadsheet for reporting and analysis.
intro: Copying form responses into a spreadsheet by hand is slow and error-prone. This guide shows how to connect Typeform with Microsoft Excel so new submissions flow into your workbook automatically, keeping your data organized for reporting and analysis.

steps.heading: Connect Typeform and Excel
steps.body: Use Typeform's built-in integration or an automation tool to link your Typeform account with your Microsoft Excel workbook.

steps.heading: Configure field mapping
steps.body: Match each Typeform question to the correct column in your Excel spreadsheet so responses land in the right place.

steps.heading: Test the automation
steps.body: Submit a test response to your form and confirm it appears correctly in Excel before relying on the integration for real submissions.

steps.heading: Verify responses in Excel
steps.body: Check your spreadsheet after live responses come in to make sure the data stays accurate and properly organized over time.

faq.question: Does this update Excel automatically for every new response?
faq.answer: Yes, once the integration is set up, new form submissions are sent to Excel automatically without manual data entry.

faq.question: Do I need Excel Online or can I use a desktop file?
faq.answer: This kind of integration generally works with a cloud-based workbook, such as one stored in OneDrive, rather than a purely local Excel file.

faq.question: What happens if I add a new question to my Typeform later?
faq.answer: You'll likely need to update the field mapping so the new question's responses are directed to the correct column in your spreadsheet.

faq.question: Can I use this data for reporting?
faq.answer: Yes, having responses automatically organized in Excel makes it easier to build reports, charts, and summaries from your form data.
